title: Advanced Systems for Seismic Isolation in Bridge Structures.
author: Kallesha K G
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Bridge structures are vital components of modern infrastructure, yet they are highly vulnerable to damage from seismic events. Traditional design approaches often focus on structural strength and ductility, which can lead to significant, though non-catastrophic, damage during a major earthquake. This paper reviews the development and application of advanced seismic isolation systems as a superior alternative for enhancing bridge resilience. By decoupling the superstructure from the ground motion, these systems reduce the transfer of seismic energy and forces, minimizing structural damage and ensuring functionality after an event. The article explores the fundamental principles of seismic isolation, examines cutting-edge technologies and materials such as high-damping rubber bearings and friction pendulum systems, and presents hypothetical case studies to illustrate their effectiveness. Furthermore, it delves into future trends, including the integration of smart materials and adaptive control systems, and identifies key challenges and opportunities in the field. The findings suggest that advanced seismic isolation represents a paradigm shift in earthquake-resistant bridge design, offering a path toward safer, more resilient, and more cost-effective infrastructure.
